subject: Mathematics
title: Scaling and Similarity
description: A 60-second lesson on Scaling and Similarity.

hook.text: Enlarge a photo to twice its size and the shapes stay the same. Only the measurements change.

buildup.text: Scaling multiplies every length by the same factor. Angles remain unchanged.

discovery.text: If you double every length, the area multiplies by 4. Triple it, and area grows by 9.

twist.text: Volume scales even faster: double the length and volume multiplies by 8.

climax.text: This is why ants can carry 50 times their weight but elephants can't—scaling changes everything.

punchline.text: Bigger isn't just more—it's a different kind of more.

quiz.question: If you double every length of a shape, what happens to the area?
